Criteria: Dangerously cold air temperatures or wind chill values are expected or occurring (typically reaching -25°F or colder depending on regional climatological thresholds). Frostbite can occur on exposed skin in minutes; poses an immediate threat to life.
Conditions are favorable for an extreme cold or dangerous wind chill event to meet or exceed warning criteria within the next 24 to 72 hours.

Cold air temperatures or wind chill values are expected to be remarkably low but welcome sub-warning thresholds (typically 0°F to -24°F depending on region). Frostbite and hypothermia are likely with prolonged, unprotected exposure.
Sub-freezing temperatures (32°F or below) are imminent or highly likely during the locally defined growing season, threatening to kill crops, outdoor vegetation, and burst unprotected plumbing.
Conditions are favorable for a significant, widespread freezing event to occur within the next 24 to 48 hours during the growing season.
Widespread frost development is expected when temperatures drop to 33°F-36°F on clear, calm nights during the growing season, capable of damaging sensitive plants.
Local emergency management or mayoral declaration triggering the activation of municipal warming shelters, suspension of utility shut-offs, and mandated safety checks for vulnerable populations.
